mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack increases recession probability, causing equity risk premiums to rise and S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal a synchronized consumer and housing demand contraction, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production indicates supply-side contraction that immediately pressures equity prices downward over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles do not correlate with negative 5-day forward returns, yielding a correlation coefficient greater than -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation runs above 3.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, real rates turn deeply negative, triggering inflation risk aversion that suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of high inflation and low nominal rates fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with a hit rate below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day forward returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.0%, corporate profit margins compress due to weak aggregate demand, causing S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while employment rises signals productivity deterioration and rising unit labor costs, which immediately pressures equity valuations downward over the following week.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-PAYEMS divergence fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 45%.
